title: Polynomials
subject: math
audioScript: Good morning, everyone. Today we're opening Polynomials. Forget the heavy textbook tone for a moment — think of this chapter as a story about Zeros of a polynomial, Relationship between zeros and coefficients, and Division algorithm for polynomials. I'll use everyday examples first, then we'll connect them to what CBSE expects in the exam.

segments.teleprompter: Good morning, everyone. Today we're opening Polynomials. Forget the heavy textbook tone for a moment — think of this chapter as a story about Zeros of a polynomial, Relationship between zeros and coefficients, and Division algorithm for polynomials. I'll use everyday examples first, then we'll connect them to what CBSE expects in the exam.

Let's lock in the must-know words for Polynomials. Don't memorise long paragraphs — learn what each term means in one simple sentence. If you can explain these words to a friend, you're already halfway there.
In this part — core concepts — we'll keep it conversational. Chapter: Polynomials. I'll connect the idea to something you've seen in daily life before we go exam-deep.
Formulas can feel scary, but they're just shortcuts. For Polynomials, I'll say what each symbol means in plain language, then show when to use it — not just how to copy it from the board.
In this part — solved examples — we'll keep it conversational. Chapter: Polynomials. I'll connect the idea to something you've seen in daily life before we go exam-deep.
In this part — common mistakes — we'll keep it conversational. Chapter: Polynomials. I'll connect the idea to something you've seen in daily life before we go exam-deep.

Look at the diagram on screen. Trace it with your finger while I talk. For Polynomials, the picture is worth more than a page of notes — notice labels, direction arrows, and what changes from left to right.

Time to practise — not to panic. I've lined up about 9 questions on Polynomials, covering Zeros of a polynomial, Relationship between zeros and coefficients, and Division algorithm for polynomials. Cover your notes, attempt each question in exam order, then check against the sections above.

Let's connect Polynomials to real life — not just the textbook. I'll walk through everyday examples for Zeros of a polynomial, Relationship between zeros and coefficients, and Division algorithm for polynomials: kitchen, lab, transport, or things you see in your neighbourhood.

Before you press play on any video about Polynomials, grab a notebook. Pause every two minutes and write one sentence in your own words. The links here are curated for Class 10 CBSE — NCERT-style explainers and one-shot revision.

This is your recap if you watched a video on Polynomials — or if you prefer reading first. I'll walk through the lesson flow: Zeros of a polynomial, Relationship between zeros and coefficients, and Division algorithm for polynomials, one common mistake, and what you should be able to explain without notes afterward.
Imagine I'm sitting beside you before the board exam. Here's Polynomials the way I'd explain it to a student who said 'I read it but didn't get it.' Slow pace, simple words, one idea at a time.
Quick recap of Polynomials. Three things to remember tonight: the big idea, one formula or rule, and one mistake to avoid. That's enough for a confident revision.

sections.title: 8. Practice Questions
sections.body:
_Class 10 CBSE practice for **Polynomials**._

**Additional practice set**

- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Zeros of a polynomial** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Polynomials.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Zeros of a polynomial** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Relationship between zeros and coefficients** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Polynomials.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Relationship between zeros and coefficients** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Division algorithm for polynomials** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Polynomials.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Division algorithm for polynomials** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **NUMERICAL (3 marks)** — Using **For ax^2 + bx + c: sum of zeros = -b/a**, solve a standard board-style problem from Polynomials. Show all working steps.
- **NUMERICAL (3 marks)** — Using **Product of zeros = c/a**, solve a standard board-style problem from Polynomials. Show all working steps.
- **LONG (5 marks)** — Long answer: Describe the main ideas of **Polynomials**. Cover at least three syllabus topics and one real-life application.

sections.title: 11. Video Transcript Summary
sections.body:
_Teacher recap — what a strong Class 10 video lesson on **Polynomials** should cover:_

**Lesson flow (as if narrated on screen):**

1. **Hook** — Start with a daily-life example tied to Polynomials, not a textbook definition.
2. **Zeros of a polynomial** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
3. **Relationship between zeros and coefficients** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
4. **Division algorithm for polynomials** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
5. **Formulas** — Write For ax^2 + bx + c: sum of zeros = -b/a on the board, explain each symbol, then solve one board-style numerical.
6. **Common mistake** — Warn about the error students make most often in exams.
7. **Recap** — Three bullet points to remember before moving to practice questions.

**After watching, you should be able to:**

- Explain Zeros of a polynomial in everyday language.
- Draw or describe one diagram related to Polynomials.
- Attempt at least two short-answer questions without notes.

**Videos linked for this chapter:**

- 🔍 [CBSE class 10 Polynomials NCERT explained Hindi English](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=CBSE+class+10+Polynomials+NCERT+explained+Hindi+English)
- 🔍 [Polynomials class 10 one shot revision](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=Polynomials+class+10+one+shot+revision)
- 🔍 [NCERT Mathematics class 10 Polynomials animation](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=NCERT+Mathematics+class+10+Polynomials+animation)

I'll walk through the lesson flow: Zeros of a polynomial, Relationship between zeros and coefficients, and Division algorithm for polynomials, one common mistake, and what you should be able to explain without notes afterward.
